lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Welcome to my discount camel store. Can I help you with anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A discount camel store?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Yes - it's my great idea - selling camels at discounted prices so that the common man can experience the joys of owning a camel of their very own! They're not just a source of kebab meat you know!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So can I buy a camel then? I'm hungry!

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: I would never sell one to the likes of you! They are beautiful, majestic creatures, not food! Get out of my shop.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, camels are beautiful creatures.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Ah, wondrous camels! Best of all Saradomin's creations. Ships of the desert, far more useful than any horse or donkey. Truly, they are a man's best friend.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Perhaps you'd like a camel of your own? You're in the right place, if so...

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No but thanks for your time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why would I want a camel? All they do is spit and...

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Pah! I've seen mummies with more brains than you! If you treat camels badly then of course they're going to spit at you. May your robes never be clean again!

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I think I was a camel in a previous life.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: You're teasing me now! You rogue!

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Everyone laughs at the poor Discount Camel Seller.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So is the discount camel business good then?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Business is booming! Pollnivneach has never seen this number of visitors.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: In fact I'm thinking of expanding and trying to break into the camel hire market.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: If only I could figure out how to get people to return the camels...

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about this town.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Well, Pollnivneach is a funny little place. It's a small town and you'd think it'd be nice and quiet.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: But those Menaphites and bandits are making the place quite unbearable.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In what way?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Well, one group is as bad as the other! They steal, they fight, they cause trouble with us locals. Just yesterday one of those bandits came looking for protection money!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why don't the authorities stop this?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: We're a small town. We don't have the resources to keep the gangs off our backs! Menaphos and Al Kharid are no help either.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: To tell the truth we live in hope that an outsider, someone with no ties to either group, could broker a deal to halt the hostilities between them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm...

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well the weak will always be trodden on. Why not stand up to them?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: That's a little unfair, my friend. We can't all be brave adventurers. If we were, where would you purchase quality camels at discounted prices?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: You might think about that. Anyway, isn't it the job of the brave adventurer to protect the weak and innocent from evil and harm?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps I could help.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Yes, perhaps you could.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know the politics better than I do. What do you recommend?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Me?! I am but a humble discount camel seller. You're the adventurer, don't you know what to do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course I know what to do! I was just testing you!

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: That is reassuring to hear. Too often one meets brainless adventurers whose solution to every problem is to hack, maim and murder.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: I'm glad to know you're not of that ilk.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps I should know, but offered advice should never be shunned.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: The state of adventurers today. They need everything spelled out for them.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: What, you want me to put a little note in your quest journal?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: But fine, I'll give you a hint. Try and see if you can get the gangs to agree to a truce by offering them each a token gift as an act of reconciliation.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: If that fails to work, you must consider trying to influence the gangs from within.

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lovely day isn't it?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: It's a beautiful day today, but word has it that it will rain tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: No! When have you ever known it to rain here in Gielinor?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh. Anyway...

lines.speaker: Ali the Camel Man
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Sami the Camel Man
lines.text: Hello! Welcome back to my discount camel store. How are those camels getting along?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Sami!

lines.speaker: Sami the Camel Man
lines.text: Hello adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any news?

lines.speaker: Sami the Camel Man
lines.text: Not much really, I just want to thank you for what you have done for this town. I might even stick around now that business is better!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any grand plans?

lines.speaker: Sami the Camel Man
lines.text: Well I've been debating whether I should diversify into camel rental, too. Although I do have all these cages lying around... do you think there's a market for discount jackals?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe! Best of luck, in any case.

lines.speaker: Sami the Camel Man
lines.text: Thank you.
